Define each saga step as an object with three things: a name, an execute function (forward action), and a compensate function (undo action). Store steps in an ordered array.
Execute steps sequentially. After each successful step, store its result in the saga context so subsequent steps can access it. If a step fails, record which step index failed.

For compensation, iterate backwards from (failedStepIndex - 1) to 0. The failed step itself does not need compensation since it did not complete. Crucially, do NOT stop if a compensation step fails - continue compensating the remaining steps.

Track the saga lifecycle with a state machine: STARTED -> EXECUTING -> COMPLETED/COMPENSATING -> COMPENSATED/COMPENSATION_FAILED. Log every transition for auditability and debugging in distributed systems.
